title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
summary: Baseten's research team discusses techniques for solving the memory problem in long-horizon agents by compressing the KV cache to enable near-lossless retrieval of relevant context at inference time. The approach uses amortization, sparse autoencoders, and iterative compaction to reduce memory overhead.
writeup: Baseten's research team presents techniques for the memory problem in long-horizon agents, where accumulated context strains inference. The core method compresses the KV cache to allow near-lossless retrieval of relevant context at inference time, drawing on amortization, sparse autoencoders, and iterative compaction to cut memory overhead. The talk ties these methods to KV cache management, test-time compute, sparse attention, and context handling for agents.
